content.cn_text: 你会听到两个工程专业的学生，一个六年级的女生叫琳达，一个五年级的男生叫马修，讨论学生工作实习的好处。
content.en_text: You will hear two engineering students, a woman in her sixth year called Linda and a man in his fifth year called Matthew, discussing the benefits of student work placements.

content.cn_text: 嗨,琳达。
content.en_text: Hi Linda.

content.cn_text: 你能抽出几分钟吗?
content.en_text: Can you spare a few minutes?

content.cn_text: 你好，马修，没问题。
content.en_text: Hello Matthew, no problem.

content.cn_text: 我只是想跟你谈谈临时工作实习…我从来没想过有什么好理由去做。
content.en_text: I just wanted to talk to you about temporary work placements ... I've never really thought there was a good reason for doing one.

content.cn_text: 我有一些积蓄，所以我现在不太需要钱。
content.en_text: I've got some savings, so I don't really need the money at the moment.

content.cn_text: 但是我收到一封来自大学的电子邮件，说有一个职位看起来很有趣。
content.en_text: But I've had an email from the university about a vacancy that looks quite interesting.

content.cn_text: 你去年实习过，对吧?
content.en_text: You did a placement last year didn't you?

content.cn_text: 是的。
content.en_text: I did, yes.

content.cn_text: 就我而言，在我开始申请长期工作之前，我想弄清楚我是否做出了正确的职业选择。
content.en_text: In my case I wanted to find out if I was making the right career choice before I began applying for permanent jobs.

content.cn_text: 我想我想在汽车制造业工作，但我不确定。
content.en_text: I thought I wanted to work in car manufacturing but I wasn't sure.

content.cn_text: 所以我申请了丰田。
content.en_text: So I applied to Toyota.

content.cn_text: 申请过程是怎样的?
content.en_text: What was the application process like?

content.cn_text: 冗长的。
content.en_text: Lengthy.

content.cn_text: 它有很多不同的部分。
content.en_text: There were a lot of different parts to it.

content.cn_text: 最无聊的是心理测试——你知道，你必须回答一大堆关于你自己的问题。
content.en_text: The dullest one was a psychometric test- you know, when you have to answer loads of questions about yourself.

content.cn_text: 你在猜什么是最好的回答!
content.en_text: And you're trying to guess what's the best thing to say!

content.cn_text: 是的。
content.en_text: Yes.

content.cn_text: 然后我们有一个小组活动，我觉得非常有趣。
content.en_text: Then there was an activity that we did in groups, which I found really fascinating.

content.cn_text: 工程师是出了名的不爱交际，但我觉得我们是一个很棒的团队。
content.en_text: Engineers are renowned for being a bit unsociable, but I thought we made a great team.

content.cn_text: 我们也有单独的任务。
content.en_text: And we had an individual task too.

content.cn_text: 我们必须对各种各样的商业文件进行分类，分清轻重缓急。
content.en_text: We had to sort through various business documents and prioritise them.

content.cn_text: 这就像你作为一名学生要做的事情，只是内容不同。
content.en_text: It was just like what you have to do as a student really, just with different content.

content.cn_text: 你到底在做什么?
content.en_text: What exactly were you doing on the placement?

content.cn_text: 我帮助设计了一些诊断软件来识别汽车装配过程中的任何浪费
content.en_text: I was helping to design some diagnostic software to identify any waste in the car assembly process

content.cn_text: 你是说浪费材料吗?
content.en_text: Do you mean waste of materials?

content.cn_text: 不,时间。
content.en_text: No, time.

content.cn_text: 任何能加快流程的方法都有助于降低成本。
content.en_text: Anything that can speed the process up helps to cut costs.

content.cn_text: 实习工作和学生生活相比怎么样?
content.en_text: How did the work placement compare to being a student?

content.cn_text: 工作辛苦吗?
content.en_text: Was it hard work?

content.cn_text: 是的，它是。
content.en_text: Yes, it was.

content.cn_text: 我以前有过全职工作——我在大学假期里做过各种不需要技能的工作，其中一些工作时间很长——所以我觉得这份工作很容易。
content.en_text: I'd had full-time work before - I've done various unskilled jobs during university holidays, and some of those involved long hours - so I thought I'd find it easy.

content.cn_text: 但我错了。
content.en_text: I was wrong though.

content.cn_text: 我认为当你在实习时，你总是试图证明自己……
content.en_text: I think when you're on placement you're always trying to prove yourself ...

content.cn_text: 所以你努力逼自己成功?
content.en_text: So you push yourself hard to succeed?

content.cn_text: 是的。
content.en_text: Yes.

content.cn_text: 但我从我的雇主那里得到了很多支持。
content.en_text: But I got a lot of support from my employers.

content.cn_text: 他们总是乐于助人。
content.en_text: They were always helpful.

content.cn_text: 然后在实习结束时，我得到了正式的反馈。
content.en_text: And then at the end of the placement I was given formal feedback.

content.cn_text: 你是说你的工程能力吗?
content.en_text: Do you mean on your engineering ability?

content.cn_text: 嗯，不，我真的不需要，因为我们每隔一天就有团队会议，所以我有机会讨论技术问题，并询问任何不清楚的问题。
content.en_text: Well, no, I didn't really need that because we had team meetings every other day, and so I had the chance to discuss technical issues and ask about anything that wasn't clear.

content.cn_text: 评估是关于一般工作场所的事情，比如组织能力、主动性……诸如此类的事情……
content.en_text: The evaluation was about general workplace things, like organisational ability, initiative... That sort of thing ...

content.cn_text: 我的印象是你认为你从实习中获益了……？
content.en_text: I get the impression you think you benefited from the placement ... ?

content.cn_text: 嗯，最好的是他们给了我一份明年的工作!
content.en_text: Well the best thing is that they've offered me a job for next year!

content.cn_text: 当然要看我的考试成绩，不过……
content.en_text: Depending on my exam results of course, but still ...

content.cn_text: 永久的?
content.en_text: A permanent one?

content.cn_text: 是的!
content.en_text: Yes!

content.cn_text: 但除此之外，我学到了很多。
content.en_text: But apart from that I learned so much .

content.cn_text: 工业环境比学术环境要求高得多，所以我的综合技能得到了提高。
content.en_text: The industrial environment was much more demanding than the academic one, so my general skills improved.

content.cn_text: 比如时间管理……赶上最后期限……在技术方面，我学到了像MS Project这样的新软件包。
content.en_text: Like time Management... meeting deadlines ... And on the technical side I learned new software packages like MS Project.

content.cn_text: 嗯，我想你已经让我相信实习工作是值得的。
content.en_text: Well, I think you've convinced me that work placements are worthwhile ...

content.cn_text: 既然你来了，你能给我点别的建议吗?
content.en_text: But while you're here can you give me advice on something else?

content.cn_text: 我要开始讲工程材料模块了，我这里有一个书单，你能快速看一下，告诉我你会推荐什么吗，如果你还记得的话?
content.en_text: I'm about to make a start on the Engineering Materials module, and I've got a booklist here - can you have a quick look and tell me what you would recommend - that's if you can remember?

content.cn_text: 让我看看……
content.en_text: Let's see ...

content.cn_text: 我确实记得其中一些……
content.en_text: I do remember some of them ...

content.cn_text: 是的，这个……材料科学。一般来说，我觉得这门学科很难，但这本书很容易理解，所以很适合我。
content.en_text: Yes, this one ... The Science of Materials.I found the subject quite hard generally, but this book is very accessible so it suited me.

content.cn_text: 但它并没有涵盖所有内容……
content.en_text: It doesn't cover everything though ...

content.cn_text: 那这个呢?
content.en_text: What about this one then .

content.cn_text: 材料工程?
content.en_text: Materials Engineering?

content.cn_text: 哦，是的，我记得。
content.en_text: Oh yes, I do remember that.

content.cn_text: 但它现在有点过时了，不是吗，除非它是一个新的版本?
content.en_text: But it's a bit out-of-date now isn't it, unless it's a new edition?

content.cn_text: 我不这么想。
content.en_text: I don't think so ...

content.cn_text: 但我喜欢的是它的图片。
content.en_text: But what I liked about it were the pictures.

content.cn_text: 他们真的帮助我理解了这些描述。
content.en_text: They really helped to understand the descriptions.

content.cn_text: 从这个角度来看，它是有用的……让我看看……还有什么？
content.en_text: It's useful just from that point of view ... Let's see ... What else?

content.cn_text: 哦，是的……那一门——工程基础——我认为在所有这些课程中，q29覆盖范围最广……
content.en_text: Oh yes ...That one there - Engineering Basics - I think out of all these that's got the widest coverage ...

content.cn_text: 但我看了目录页，几乎没有提到纳米技术。
content.en_text: But I've looked at the contents page, and it hardly mentions nanotechnology.

content.cn_text: 是的，你说得对。
content.en_text: Yes, you're right.

content.cn_text: 但《材料的进化》就有。
content.en_text: The Evolution of Materials does though.

content.cn_text: 这是最近出版的，所以它涵盖了所有最新的发展。
content.en_text: It's a recent publication so it covers all the latest developments.

content.cn_text: 不过，在20世纪60年代，它的数量有点少，而那十年非常重要。
content.en_text: It's a bit thin on the nineteen sixties though,and that decade was quite important.

content.cn_text: 听起来好像它们在某些方面是互补的。
content.en_text: Well it sounds as if they all complement each other in some ways.

content.cn_text: 我想你不能借给我。
content.en_text: I don't suppose you can lend me .

groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: Why is Matthew considering a student work placement?

options.title: A
options.content: He was informed about an interesting vacancy.

options.title: C
options.content: He wants to try out a career option.

items.analysis: Matthew考虑实习的原因是他收到了一封来自大学的邮件，其中提到了一个看起来很有趣的职位空缺。这直接对应了选项A，表明他是因为被告知了一个有趣的职位空缺而考虑实习的。文中Matthew明确表示他之前从未真正考虑过实习的好处，但这封邮件引起了他的兴趣，这正是他开始考虑实习的原因。

items.title: Which part of the application process did Linda find most interesting?
items.analysis: Linda在描述申请过程的不同部分时，特别强调了小组活动，使用了"really fascinating"这个词组来表达她的兴趣。这是她对所有申请过程部分中唯一使用如此积极词汇的描述，明确表明她对这部分最感兴趣。相比之下，她对其他部分的描述要么是中性的，要么带有一些负面色彩。因此，小组活动是Linda在申请过程中最感兴趣的部分。

items.title: During her work placement, Linda helped find ways to

options.title: C
options.content: calculate the cost of design faults

items.analysis: Linda在谈论她的工作实习经历时，明确提到她在设计诊断软件，目的是识别汽车装配过程中的浪费。她进一步解释说，加速这个过程可以帮助降低成本。这直接对应了选项A中的"speed up car assembly"（加速汽车装配）。虽然原文中提到了"waste"（浪费），但Linda澄清这里指的是时间浪费，而不是材料浪费。因此，正确答案是A。

items.title: Why did Linda find her work placement tiring?

options.title: A
options.content: She wasn't used to full-time work.

options.title: B
options.content: The working hours were very long.

options.title: C
options.content: She felt she had to prove her worth

items.analysis: Linda在讨论她的工作实习经历时，直接提到了实习期间感到疲惫的原因。她强调当处于实习岗位时，总是在努力证明自己的能力。这种持续证明自我价值的压力是导致她感到疲惫的主要原因，而不是工作时间长或不适应全职工作。这与选项C "She felt she had to prove her worth" 直接对应，因此C是正确答案。

items.title: What did Linda's employers give her formal feedback on?
items.analysis: 文中明确提到评估（evaluation）是关于一般工作场所的事情，并特别列举了组织能力（organisational ability）作为例子。这直接对应了选项B中的"organisational skills"。而其他选项提到的工程能力和团队合作并未在这段评估描述中被提及。因此，B选项是最符合原文内容的答案。

items.title: What was the main benefit of Linda's work placement?

options.title: C
options.content: The opportunity to use new software.

items.analysis: 文中Linda明确表示"the best thing"（最好的事情）是获得了工作offer，这直接对应了题目中询问的"main benefit"（主要好处）。她还强调这是一份永久性工作，进一步突出了这一好处的重要性。虽然Linda也提到了其他好处，如技能提升和学习新软件，但她明确将工作offer称为"the best thing"，因此这是她认为的主要好处。

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What does Linda think about the books on Matthew's reading list?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to questions 27-30.
Books

items.title: The Science of Materials
items.analysis: Linda提到《材料科学》这本书时，用了"accessible"这个词来描述，意味着这本书容易理解。她还补充说"it suited me"（它很适合我），进一步强调了这本书对学生来说是易于理解的。因此，选项B "easy to understand"（易于理解）最准确地描述了Linda对这本书的看法。

items.title: Materials Engineering
items.analysis: 文中提到Linda喜欢这本书的图片，并且这些图片有助于理解书中的描述。这直接对应了选项A "helpful illustrations"（有帮助的插图）。图片（pictures）在这里可以理解为插图（illustrations），而"helped to understand"（有助于理解）则表明这些插图是有帮助的（helpful）。因此，A选项最准确地描述了Linda对这本书的看法。

items.title: Engineering Basics
items.analysis: 原文中Linda提到Engineering Basics "got the widest coverage"，意为"涵盖范围最广"。这与选项D "comprehensive"（全面的）在含义上是一致的。"comprehensive"通常用来描述内容全面、涵盖广泛的书籍或资料，因此D选项最符合Linda对这本书的评价。

items.title: Evolution of Materials
items.analysis: 文中Linda提到"Evolution of Materials"是"recent publication"（最新出版），并且"covers all the latest developments"（涵盖了所有最新的发展）。这两个描述直接对应了选项C "up-to-date"（最新的，与时俱进的）的含义。因此，C选项是正确答案。
